purpose: Tracks the current official-tree comparison and convergence classification progress for codex/.

completed: The Codex subtree is synchronized through the current official commit with only the six documented retained seams.
All 125 local-only paths are classified; generated artifacts and tests follow their owning source seam.
Profile lifecycle, encrypted credentials, authorization, durable approvals, Git workspaces, Run leases, browser DTOs, and event delivery are owned by apps/web.
The browser uses typed REST and authenticated WebSocket transport; Tauri, desktop sidecars, raw browser RPC, desktop release chains, and desktop build dependencies are absent.
Provider configuration and onboarding implementation live in three dedicated TUI modules; high-churn upstream dispatcher and auth files retain only narrow attachments.
Generated contracts reproduce without drift and the current binary passes the real app-server Capability Manifest smoke.

validation.clippyFix: passed for codex-tui; one non-blocking vec_init_then_push warning remains
validation.chatTransportTests: 174 codex-api tests and 17 focused Core Chat tests passed
validation.providerTests: 26 Provider metadata, 57 Provider transport, 41 model-manager, 27 focused Core Provider, and 5 app-server model-list tests passed
validation.appServerTests: focused model-list suite passed; the real initialize smoke passed against the locally built CLI
validation.networkProxyTests: all 207 network-proxy tests passed across local-port and network-isolated runs; the new CLI sandbox cases are Linux-only and not applicable on the macOS validation host
validation.platformTests: frontend, Rust workspace, PostgreSQL integration, Profile restart, Provider isolation, Git Runtime, Run lease, two-organization security, and fake-server HTTP/WebSocket checks passed

progress.nextAction: Keep the six retained seams replayable at the next official sync; product work continues with authorized per-user dynamic Profile routing and the remaining M2 session and Runner hardening gates.
